warps.notes: Left door tile back to Route 27 (tile 33,7)
Right door tile back to Route 27 (tile 33,7)

tile_layout.notes: Standard 4x4 block house interior (House1.blk = 16 bytes). Bookshelf on top row. Granny NPC gives TM37 Sandstorm if lead Pokemon happiness > 150.

npcs.name: Granny (TM giver)
npcs.dialogue_summary: Checks if player is heading to the Pokemon League. Checks lead Pokemon happiness. If happiness > 150: gives TM37 Sandstorm and describes it as a move for advanced trainers only. If happiness <= 150: warns that the Pokemon doesn't trust the player enough yet. After receiving TM: describes TM37 Sandstorm (damages both battlers, advanced trainers only).
npcs.notes: Uses special GetFirstPokemonHappiness. Happiness threshold: ifgreater 149 (> 150 means >= 151 in game logic). No palette override (uses default).

items.name: TM37 Sandstorm
items.notes: Given by Granny NPC, not a standalone item ball. One-time gift.

events.description: If lead Pokemon has happiness >= 151: granny gives TM37 Sandstorm (one-time). Event flag EVENT_GOT_TM37_SANDSTORM prevents re-triggering.
If lead Pokemon has happiness <= 150: granny tells player the Pokemon doesn't trust them enough, and emphasizes trust between trainer and Pokemon.
Granny describes TM37 Sandstorm: inflicts damage on both battlers, for advanced trainers only.

notes: Small 4x4 block interior (8x8 tiles) on Route 27 at tile (33, 7). A Granny NPC gives TM37 Sandstorm as a happiness-gated gift. The happiness check uses GetFirstPokemonHappiness and requires the lead Pokemon to have happiness strictly greater than 150 (>= 151 in game terms). This is one of very few happiness-gated TM rewards in the game. Two bookshelf bg_events on the top row use MagazineBookshelfScript. The house is thematically appropriate as a waypoint between Johto and Kanto — the Sandstorm TM's aggressive theme contrasts with the trust-based requirement to receive it.
